formatted_address: 6310 Laguna Bay Ct, Houston, TX 77041, USA
pitch_rationale: The homes at this Houston, TX location feature multi-faceted hip roofs with no prominent gables or dormers. Shadow lengths cast from the eaves onto surrounding hardscape measure approximately 8–12 feet using the 50-ft scale bar, consistent with a moderate ~5:12 pitch given estimated eave heights of 12–14 ft. The visible roof planes are broad relative to the overall footprint but show clear tonal gradation between faces, ruling out a very shallow 3:12–4:12 or a steep 7:12+ pitch. The southern Houston climate strongly favors 4:12–6:12 pitches, and the roof-to-eave geometry with ridges sitting moderately inward from the eave perimeter best matches a 5:12 estimate.
footprint_rationale: Subject is the large, multi-section all-hip roofed home in the center of the image, identified by its freeform pool to the east. Bounding box estimated at ~365×290 px (77.5×61.6 ft = 4,775 sq ft plan area). Applied ~66% fill factor to account for the complex re-entrant inter-wing cutouts typical of this multi-pyramid hip roof style, yielding ~3,152 sq ft footprint. Five ridge segments identified atop each main hip section (~295 px total). Approximately 14 diagonal hip edges traced from ridge ends/peaks to eave corners (~910 px). Five valley lines identified at interior intersections of roof planes (~270 px). No rake edges present (all-hip construction). Eave perimeter estimated by tracing lower horizontal edges around the complex plan (~800 px). Confidence is moderate-low due to roof complexity, steep viewing angle, and difficulty distinguishing individual section boundaries.
